name: EA-CORE Ontology (Enterprise Architecture Core)
description: Platform-agnostic enterprise architecture core ontology providing foundational EA domains (Business, Data, Application, Technology, Security, Integration), architecture governance entities, and universal relationships for capability mapping, decision recording, and roadmap planning.

components.EAEntity: Base class for all enterprise architecture entities
components.DataArchitecture: Data Architecture domain for information assets
components.ApplicationArchitecture: Application Architecture domain for software systems
components.BusinessCapability: Organisational ability or capacity to achieve outcomes
components.BusinessProcess: Value-creating activity collection within a domain
components.DataEntity: Fundamental business data object or information asset
components.ArchitectureDecision: Recorded decision with rationale and implications
components.CapabilityGap: Gap between current and target capability state

crossOntology.name: managedBy
crossOntology.purpose: EA-CORE → ORG: Entity ownership and management accountability

crossOntology.name: alignsTo
crossOntology.purpose: EA-CORE → VSOM: Strategic alignment of architecture entities

versionHistory.changes: Initial platform-agnostic EA core ontology consolidated from V3 source material. 15 entities, 12 relationships, 8 business rules, 4 enumerations. OAA v6.1.0 compliant.

notes: EA-CORE provides the platform-agnostic foundation for enterprise architecture modelling. Designed for extension by platform-specific ontologies (EA-MSFT, future EA-AWS, EA-GCP) and methodology ontologies (EA-TOGAF). Sits alongside existing EA-ONT (PPM portfolio execution layer).
